input.description: Explore codebase structure and registry patterns
input.prompt:
Explore a shadcn/ui component library codebase to understand its overall structure, registry patterns, and how components are registered. I need a thorough understanding of:

1. The top-level directory structure (what folders exist, what they contain)
2. How the registry works - find registry files (e.g., registry.ts, registry/index.ts, or similar) and understand the format for registering components and examples/demos
3. The structure of the `registry` folder specifically - what kinds of entries exist (ui, examples, etc.)
4. How demo/example files are structured and named
5. Any configuration files like components.json, package.json

Please read any registry index files you find in their entirety. Also look for any existing compound components (ones that use React context, sub-components with dot notation like Component.SubComponent) to understand the patterns used.

Be thorough - read actual file contents, not just directory listings.

input.description: Explore existing compound UI components for patterns
input.prompt:
Explore a shadcn/ui component library codebase to understand how existing compound components are built. I need to understand the patterns used for:

1. Components that use React context to share state between sub-components
2. Components with sub-components accessed via dot notation (e.g., Tabs.TabsList, Accordion.AccordionItem)
3. How components handle orientation (horizontal vs vertical)
4. The TypeScript patterns used (component props interfaces, context types, forwardRef usage, displayName)
5. How CSS variables and Tailwind classes are used for styling
6. How cn() utility is used

Specifically look for and read these types of components if they exist:
- Tabs (tabs.tsx) - a good example of compound component with context
- Accordion (accordion.tsx) - another compound component
- Breadcrumb (breadcrumb.tsx) - has sub-components
- Any component with horizontal/vertical orientation support

Read the actual file contents of at least 2-3 compound components in full. Also check if there's a `lib/utils.ts` file for the `cn` utility.

The components are likely in `registry/new-york/ui/` or `components/ui/` or similar directories.

3	// Note: if you edit the schema here, you must also edit the schema in the

6	export const registryConfigItemSchema = z.union([
7	  // Simple string format: "https://example.com/{name}.json"
8	  z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
9	    message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
10	  }),
11	  // Advanced object format with auth options
12	  z.object({
13	    url: z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
14	      message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
15	    }),
16	    params: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
17	    headers: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
18	  }),
19	])
20	
21	export const registryConfigSchema = z.record(
22	  z.string().refine((key) => key.startsWith("@"), {
23	    message: "Registry names must start with @ (e.g., @v0, @acme)",
24	  }),
25	  registryConfigItemSchema
26	)
27	
28	export const rawConfigSchema = z
29	  .object({
30	    $schema: z.string().optional(),
31	    style: z.string(),
32	    rsc: z.coerce.boolean().default(false),
33	    tsx: z.coerce.boolean().default(true),
34	    tailwind: z.object({
35	      config: z.string().optional(),
36	      css: z.string(),
37	      baseColor: z.string(),
38	      cssVariables: z.boolean().default(true),
39	      prefix: z.string().default("").optional(),
40	    }),
41	    iconLibrary: z.string().optional(),
42	    rtl: z.coerce.boolean().default(false).optional(),
43	    menuColor: z
44	      .enum([
45	        "default",
46	        "inverted",
47	        "default-translucent",
48	        "inverted-translucent",
49	      ])
50	      .default("default")
51	      .optional(),
52	    menuAccent: z.enum(["subtle", "bold"]).default("subtle").optional(),
53	    aliases: z.object({
54	      components: z.string(),
55	      utils: z.string(),
56	      ui: z.string().optional(),
57	      lib: z.string().optional(),
58	      hooks: z.string().optional(),
59	    }),
60	    registries: registryConfigSchema.optional(),
61	  })
62	  .strict()
63	
64	export const configSchema = rawConfigSchema.extend({
65	  resolvedPaths: z.object({

81	export const registryItemTypeSchema = z.enum([

83	  "registry:block",
84	  "registry:component",
85	  "registry:ui",
86	  "registry:hook",
87	  "registry:page",
88	  "registry:file",
89	  "registry:theme",
90	  "registry:style",
91	  "registry:item",
92	  "registry:base",
93	  "registry:font",

95	  // Internal use only.

100	export const registryItemFileSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
101	  // Target is required for registry:file and registry:page
102	  z.object({

104	    content: z.string().optional(),
105	    type: z.enum(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),

108	  z.object({

116	export const registryItemTailwindSchema = z.object({

118	    .object({

126	export const registryItemCssVarsSchema = z.object({

129	  dark: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
130	})
131	
132	// Recursive type for CSS properties that supports empty objects at any level.
133	const cssValueSchema: z.ZodType<any> = z.lazy(() =>
134	  z.union([

37	export function resolveRegistryItemsFromRegistries(
38	  items: string[],
39	  config: Config
40	) {
41	  const registryHeaders: Record<string, Record<string, string>> = {}
42	  const resolvedItems = [...items]
43	
44	  if (!config?.registries) {
45	    setRegistryHeaders({})
46	    return resolvedItems
47	  }
48	
49	  for (let i = 0; i < resolvedItems.length; i++) {
50	    const resolved = buildUrlAndHeadersForRegistryItem(resolvedItems[i], config)
51	
52	    if (resolved) {
53	      resolvedItems[i] = resolved.url
54	
55	      if (Object.keys(resolved.headers).length > 0) {
56	        registryHeaders[resolved.url] = resolved.headers
57	      }
58	    }
59	  }
60	
61	  setRegistryHeaders(registryHeaders)
62	
63	  return resolvedItems
64	}

66	// Internal function that fetches registry items without clearing context.
67	// This is used for recursive dependency resolution.
68	export async function fetchRegistryItems(
69	  items: string[],
70	  config: Config,
71	  options: { useCache?: boolean } = {}
72	) {
73	  const results = await Promise.all(
74	    items.map(async (item) => {
75	      if (isLocalFile(item)) {
76	        return fetchRegistryLocal(item)
77	      }
78	
79	      if (isUrl(item)) {
80	        const [result] = await fetchRegistry([item], options)
81	        try {
82	          return registryItemSchema.parse(result)
83	        } catch (error) {
84	          throw new RegistryParseError(item, error)
85	        }
86	      }
87	
88	      if (item.startsWith("@") && config?.registries) {
89	        const paths = resolveRegistryItemsFromRegistries([item], config)
90	        const [result] = await fetchRegistry(paths, options)
91	        try {
92	          return registryItemSchema.parse(result)
93	        } catch (error) {
94	          throw new RegistryParseError(item, error)
95	        }
96	      }
97	
98	      const path = `styles/${config?.style ?? "new-york-v4"}/${item}.json`
99	      const [result] = await fetchRegistry([path], options)
100	      try {
101	        return registryItemSchema.parse(result)
102	      } catch (error) {
103	        throw new RegistryParseError(item, error)
104	      }
105	    })
106	  )
107	
108	  return results
109	}
110	
111	// Helper schema for items with source tracking.
112	const registryItemWithSourceSchema = registryItemCommonSchema
113	  .extend({
114	    type: registryItemTypeSchema,
115	    _source: z.string().optional(),
116	    // Optional fields for specific item types.
117	    font: registryItemFontSchema.optional(),
118	    config: z.any().optional(),
119	  })
120	  .passthrough()
121	
122	// Resolves a list of registry items with all their dependencies and returns
123	// a complete installation bundle with merged configuration.
124	export async function resolveRegistryTree(
125	  names: z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>["name"][],
126	  config: Config,
127	  options: { useCache?: boolean } = {}
128	) {
129	  options = {
130	    useCache: true,
131	    ...options,
132	  }
133	
134	  let payload: z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>[] = []
135	  let allDependencyItems: z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>[] = []
136	  let allDependencyRegistryNames: string[] = []
137	
138	  const uniqueNames = Array.from(new Set(names))
139	
140	  const results = await fetchRegistryItems(uniqueNames, config, options)
141	
142	  const resultMap = new Map<string, z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>>()
143	  for (let i = 0; i < results.length; i++) {
144	    if (results[i]) {
145	      resultMap.set(uniqueNames[i], results[i])
146	    }
147	  }
148	
149	  for (const [sourceName, item] of Array.from(resultMap.entries())) {
150	    // Add source tracking
151	    const itemWithSource: z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema> = {
152	      ...item,
153	      _source: sourceName,
154	    }
155	    payload.push(itemWithSource)
156	
157	    if (item.registryDependencies) {
158	      // Resolve namespace syntax and set headers for dependencies
159	      let resolvedDependencies = item.registryDependencies
160	
161	      // Check for namespaced dependencies when no registries are configured
162	      if (!config?.registries) {
163	        const namespacedDeps = item.registryDependencies.filter((dep: string) =>
164	          dep.startsWith("@")
165	        )
166	        if (namespacedDeps.length > 0) {
167	          const { registry } = parseRegistryAndItemFromString(namespacedDeps[0])
168	          throw new RegistryNotConfiguredError(registry)
169	        }
170	      } else {
171	        resolvedDependencies = resolveRegistryItemsFromRegistries(
172	          item.registryDependencies,
173	          config
174	        )
175	      }
176	
177	      const { items, registryNames } = await resolveDependenciesRecursively(
178	        resolvedDependencies,
179	        config,
180	        options,
181	        new Set(uniqueNames)
182	      )
183	      allDependencyItems.push(...items)
184	      allDependencyRegistryNames.push(...registryNames)
185	    }
186	  }
187	
188	  payload.push(...allDependencyItems)
189	
190	  // Handle any remaining registry names that need index resolution
191	  if (allDependencyRegistryNames.length > 0) {
192	    // Remove duplicates from registry names
193	    const uniqueRegistryNames = Array.from(new Set(allDependencyRegistryNames))
194	
195	    // Separate namespaced and non-namespaced items
196	    const nonNamespacedItems = uniqueRegistryNames.filter(
197	      (name) => !name.startsWith("@")
198	    )
199	    const namespacedDepItems = uniqueRegistryNames.filter((name) =>
200	      name.startsWith("@")
201	    )
202	
203	    // Handle namespaced dependency items
204	    if (namespacedDepItems.length > 0) {
205	      // This will now throw specific errors on failure
206	      const depResults = await fetchRegistryItems(
207	        namespacedDepItems,
208	        config,
209	        options
210	      )
211	
212	      for (let i = 0; i < depResults.length; i++) {
213	        const item = depResults[i]
214	        const itemWithSource: z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema> = {
215	          ...item,
216	          _source: namespacedDepItems[i],
217	        }
218	        payload.push(itemWithSource)
219	      }
220	    }
221	
222	    // For non-namespaced items, we need the index and style resolution
223	    if (nonNamespacedItems.length > 0) {
224	      const index = await getShadcnRegistryIndex()
225	      if (!index && payload.length === 0) {
226	        return null
227	      }
228	
229	      if (index) {
230	        // If we're resolving the index, we want it to go first
231	        if (nonNamespacedItems.includes("index")) {
232	          nonNamespacedItems.unshift("index")
233	        }
234	
235	        // Resolve non-namespaced items through the existing flow
236	        // Get URLs for all registry items including their dependencies
237	        const registryUrls: string[] = []
238	        for (const name of nonNamespacedItems) {
239	          const itemDependencies = await resolveRegistryDependencies(
240	            name,
241	            config,
242	            options
243	          )
244	          registryUrls.push(...itemDependencies)
245	        }
246	
247	        // Deduplicate URLs
248	        const uniqueUrls = Array.from(new Set(registryUrls))
249	        let result = await fetchRegistry(uniqueUrls, options)
250	        const registryPayload = z.array(registryItemSchema).parse(result)
251	        payload.push(...registryPayload)
252	      }
253	    }
254	  }
255	
256	  if (!payload.length) {
257	    return null
258	  }
259	
260	  // No deduplication - we want to support multiple items with the same name from different sources
261	
262	  // If we're resolving the index, we want to fetch
263	  // the theme item if a base color is provided.
264	  // We do this for index only.
265	  // Other components will ship with their theme tokens.
266	  if (
267	    uniqueNames.includes("index") ||
268	    allDependencyRegistryNames.includes("index")
269	  ) {
270	    if (config.tailwind.baseColor) {
271	      const theme = await registryGetTheme(config.tailwind.baseColor, config)
272	      if (theme) {
273	        payload.unshift(theme)
274	      }
275	    }
276	  }
277	
278	  // Build source map for topological sort.
279	  const sourceMap = new Map<
280	    z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>,
281	    string
282	  >()
283	  payload.forEach((item) => {
284	    // Use the _source property if it was added, otherwise use the name.
285	    const source = item._source || item.name
286	    sourceMap.set(item, source)
287	  })
288	
289	  // Apply topological sort to ensure dependencies come before dependents.
290	  payload = topologicalSortRegistryItems(payload, sourceMap)
291	
292	  // Sort the payload so that registry:theme items come first,
293	  // while maintaining the relative order of all items.
294	  payload.sort((a, b) => {
295	    if (a.type === "registry:theme" && b.type !== "registry:theme") {
296	      return -1
297	    }
298	    if (a.type !== "registry:theme" && b.type === "registry:theme") {
299	      return 1
300	    }
301	    return 0
302	  })
303	
304	  let tailwind = {}
305	  payload.forEach((item) => {
306	    tailwind = deepmerge(tailwind, item.tailwind ?? {})
307	  })
308	
309	  let cssVars = {}
310	  payload.forEach((item) => {
311	    cssVars = deepmerge(cssVars, item.cssVars ?? {})
312	  })
313	
314	  let css = {}
315	  payload.forEach((item) => {
316	    css = deepmerge(css, item.css ?? {})
317	  })
318	
319	  let docs = ""
320	  payload.forEach((item) => {
321	    if (item.docs) {
322	      docs += `${item.docs}\n`
323	    }
324	  })
325	
326	  let envVars = {}
327	  payload.forEach((item) => {
328	    envVars = deepmerge(envVars, item.envVars ?? {})
329	  })
330	
331	  // Deduplicate files based on resolved target paths.
332	  const deduplicatedFiles = await deduplicateFilesByTarget(
333	    payload.map((item) => item.files ?? []),
334	    config
335	  )
336	
337	  // Collect font items.
338	  const fonts: RegistryFontItem[] = payload
339	    .filter((item) => item.type === "registry:font" && item.font)
340	    .map((item) => ({
341	      ...item,
342	      type: "registry:font" as const,
343	      font: item.font!,
344	    }))
345	
346	  const parsed = registryResolvedItemsTreeSchema.parse({
347	    dependencies: deepmerge.all(payload.map((item) => item.dependencies ?? [])),
348	    devDependencies: deepmerge.all(
349	      payload.map((item) => item.devDependencies ?? [])
350	    ),
351	    files: deduplicatedFiles,
352	    tailwind,
353	    cssVars,
354	    css,
355	    docs,
356	    fonts: fonts.length > 0 ? fonts : undefined,
357	  })
358	
359	  if (Object.keys(envVars).length > 0) {
360	    parsed.envVars = envVars
361	  }
362	
363	  return parsed
364	}

366	async function resolveDependenciesRecursively(
367	  dependencies: string[],
368	  config: Config,
369	  options: { useCache?: boolean } = {},
370	  visited: Set<string> = new Set()
371	) {
372	  const items: z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>[] = []
373	  const registryNames: string[] = []
374	
375	  for (const dep of dependencies) {
376	    if (visited.has(dep)) {
377	      continue
378	    }
379	    visited.add(dep)
380	
381	    // Handle URLs and local files directly.
382	    if (isUrl(dep) || isLocalFile(dep)) {
383	      const [item] = await fetchRegistryItems([dep], config, options)
384	      if (item) {
385	        items.push(item)
386	        if (item.registryDependencies) {
387	          // Resolve namespaced dependencies to set proper headers.
388	          const resolvedDeps = config?.registries
389	            ? resolveRegistryItemsFromRegistries(
390	                item.registryDependencies,
391	                config
392	              )
393	            : item.registryDependencies
394	
395	          const nested = await resolveDependenciesRecursively(
396	            resolvedDeps,
397	            config,
398	            options,
399	            visited
400	          )
401	          items.push(...nested.items)
402	          registryNames.push(...nested.registryNames)
403	        }
404	      }
405	    }
406	    // Handle namespaced items (e.g., @one/foo, @two/bar).
407	    else if (dep.startsWith("@") && config?.registries) {
408	      // Check if the registry exists.
409	      const { registry } = parseRegistryAndItemFromString(dep)
410	      if (registry && !(registry in config.registries)) {
411	        throw new RegistryNotConfiguredError(registry)
412	      }
413	
414	      // Let getRegistryItem handle the namespaced item with config
415	      // This ensures proper authentication headers are used
416	      const [item] = await fetchRegistryItems([dep], config, options)
417	      if (item) {
418	        items.push(item)
419	        if (item.registryDependencies) {
420	          // Resolve namespaced dependencies to set proper headers.
421	          const resolvedDeps = config?.registries
422	            ? resolveRegistryItemsFromRegistries(
423	                item.registryDependencies,
424	                config
425	              )
426	            : item.registryDependencies
427	
428	          const nested = await resolveDependenciesRecursively(
429	            resolvedDeps,
430	            config,
431	            options,
432	            visited
433	          )
434	          items.push(...nested.items)
435	          registryNames.push(...nested.registryNames)
436	        }
437	      }
438	    }
439	    // Handle regular component names.
440	    else {
441	      registryNames.push(dep)
442	
443	      if (config) {
444	        try {
445	          const [item] = await fetchRegistryItems([dep], config, options)
446	          if (item && item.registryDependencies) {
447	            // Resolve namespaced dependencies to set proper headers.
448	            const resolvedDeps = config?.registries
449	              ? resolveRegistryItemsFromRegistries(
450	                  item.registryDependencies,
451	                  config
452	                )
453	              : item.registryDependencies
454	
455	            const nested = await resolveDependenciesRecursively(
456	              resolvedDeps,
457	              config,
458	              options,
459	              visited
460	            )
461	            items.push(...nested.items)
462	            registryNames.push(...nested.registryNames)
463	          }
464	        } catch (error) {
465	          // If we can't fetch the registry item, that's okay - we'll still
466	          // include the name.
467	        }
468	      }
469	    }
470	  }
471	
472	  return { items, registryNames }
473	}

475	async function resolveRegistryDependencies(
476	  url: string,
477	  config: Config,
478	  options: { useCache?: boolean } = {}
479	) {
480	  if (isUrl(url)) {
481	    return [url]
482	  }
483	
484	  const { registryNames } = await resolveDependenciesRecursively(
485	    [url],
486	    config,
487	    options,
488	    new Set()
489	  )
490	
491	  const style = config.resolvedPaths?.cwd
492	    ? await getTargetStyleFromConfig(config.resolvedPaths.cwd, config.style)
493	    : config.style
494	
495	  const urls = registryNames.map((name) =>
496	    resolveRegistryUrl(isUrl(name) ? name : `styles/${style}/${name}.json`)
497	  )
498	
499	  return Array.from(new Set(urls))
500	}

578	function computeItemHash(
579	  item: Pick<z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>, "name">,
580	  source?: string
581	) {
582	  const identifier = source || item.name
583	
584	  const hash = createHash("sha256")
585	    .update(identifier)
586	    .digest("hex")
587	    .substring(0, 8)
588	
589	  return `${item.name}::${hash}`
590	}
591	
592	function extractItemIdentifierFromDependency(dependency: string) {
593	  if (isUrl(dependency)) {
594	    const url = new URL(dependency)
595	    const pathname = url.pathname
596	    const match = pathname.match(/\/([^/]+)\.json$/)
597	    const name = match ? match[1] : path.basename(pathname, ".json")
598	
599	    return {
600	      name,
601	      hash: computeItemHash({ name }, dependency),
602	    }
603	  }
604	
605	  if (isLocalFile(dependency)) {
606	    const match = dependency.match(/\/([^/]+)\.json$/)
607	    const name = match ? match[1] : path.basename(dependency, ".json")
608	
609	    return {
610	      name,
611	      hash: computeItemHash({ name }, dependency),
612	    }
613	  }
614	
615	  const { item } = parseRegistryAndItemFromString(dependency)
616	  return {
617	    name: item,
618	    hash: computeItemHash({ name: item }, dependency),
619	  }
620	}
621	
622	function topologicalSortRegistryItems(
623	  items: z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>[],
624	  sourceMap: Map<z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>, string>
625	) {
626	  const itemMap = new Map<
627	    string,
628	    z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>
629	  >()
630	  const hashToItem = new Map<
631	    string,
632	    z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>
633	  >()
634	  const inDegree = new Map<string, number>()
635	  const adjacencyList = new Map<string, string[]>()
636	
637	  items.forEach((item) => {
638	    const source = sourceMap.get(item) || item.name
639	    const hash = computeItemHash(item, source)
640	
641	    itemMap.set(hash, item)
642	    hashToItem.set(hash, item)
643	    inDegree.set(hash, 0)
644	    adjacencyList.set(hash, [])
645	  })
646	
647	  // Build a map of dependency to possible items.
648	  const depToHashes = new Map<string, string[]>()
649	  items.forEach((item) => {
650	    const source = sourceMap.get(item) || item.name
651	    const hash = computeItemHash(item, source)
652	
653	    if (!depToHashes.has(item.name)) {
654	      depToHashes.set(item.name, [])
655	    }
656	    depToHashes.get(item.name)!.push(hash)
657	
658	    if (source !== item.name) {
659	      if (!depToHashes.has(source)) {
660	        depToHashes.set(source, [])
661	      }
662	      depToHashes.get(source)!.push(hash)
663	    }
664	  })
665	
666	  items.forEach((item) => {
667	    const itemSource = sourceMap.get(item) || item.name
668	    const itemHash = computeItemHash(item, itemSource)
669	
670	    if (item.registryDependencies) {
671	      item.registryDependencies.forEach((dep) => {
672	        let depHash: string | undefined
673	
674	        const exactMatches = depToHashes.get(dep) || []
675	        if (exactMatches.length === 1) {
676	          depHash = exactMatches[0]
677	        } else if (exactMatches.length > 1) {
678	          // Multiple matches - try to disambiguate.
679	          // For now, just use the first one and warn.
680	          depHash = exactMatches[0]
681	        } else {
682	          const { name } = extractItemIdentifierFromDependency(dep)
683	          const nameMatches = depToHashes.get(name) || []
684	          if (nameMatches.length > 0) {
685	            depHash = nameMatches[0]
686	          }
687	        }
688	
689	        if (depHash && itemMap.has(depHash)) {
690	          adjacencyList.get(depHash)!.push(itemHash)
691	          inDegree.set(itemHash, inDegree.get(itemHash)! + 1)
692	        }
693	      })
694	    }
695	  })
696	
697	  // Implements Kahn's algorithm.
698	  const queue: string[] = []
699	  const sorted: z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>[] = []
700	
701	  inDegree.forEach((degree, hash) => {
702	    if (degree === 0) {
703	      queue.push(hash)
704	    }
705	  })
706	
707	  while (queue.length > 0) {
708	    const currentHash = queue.shift()!
709	    const item = itemMap.get(currentHash)!
710	    sorted.push(item)
711	
712	    adjacencyList.get(currentHash)!.forEach((dependentHash) => {
713	      const newDegree = inDegree.get(dependentHash)! - 1
714	      inDegree.set(dependentHash, newDegree)
715	
716	      if (newDegree === 0) {
717	        queue.push(dependentHash)
718	      }
719	    })
720	  }
721	
722	  if (sorted.length !== items.length) {
723	    // console.warn("Circular dependency detected in registry items")
724	    // Return all items even if there are circular dependencies
725	    // Items not in sorted are part of circular dependencies
726	    const sortedHashes = new Set(
727	      sorted.map((item) => {
728	        const source = sourceMap.get(item) || item.name
729	        return computeItemHash(item, source)
730	      })
731	    )
732	
733	    items.forEach((item) => {
734	      const source = sourceMap.get(item) || item.name
735	      const hash = computeItemHash(item, source)
736	      if (!sortedHashes.has(hash)) {
737	        sorted.push(item)
738	      }
739	    })
740	  }
741	
742	  return sorted
743	}

1	#!/bin/bash
2	
3	set -e # bail on errors
4	GLOB=$1
5	IS_CI="${CI:-false}"
6	BASE=$(pwd)
7	COMMIT_MESSAGE=$(git log -1 --pretty=%B)
8	
9	for folder in $GLOB; do
10	  [ -d "$folder" ] || continue
11	  cd $BASE
12	
13	  if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]; then
14	    git add .
15	    git commit -m "chore: update template"
16	    git push origin main
17	  fi
18	
19	  NAME=${folder##*/}
20	  CLONE_DIR="__${NAME}__clone__"
21	
22	  # sync to read-only clones
23	  # clone, delete files in the clone, and copy (new) files over
24	  # this handles file deletions, additions, and changes seamlessly
25	  # note: redirect output to dev/null to avoid any possibility of leaking token
26	  git clone --quiet --depth 1 git@github.com:shadcn/${NAME}.git $CLONE_DIR > /dev/null
27	  cd $CLONE_DIR
28	  find . | grep -v ".git" | grep -v "^\.*$" | xargs rm -rf # delete all files (to handle deletions in monorepo)
29	  cp -r $BASE/$folder/. .
30	
31	  if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]; then
32	    git add .
33	    git commit -m "$COMMIT_MESSAGE"
34	    git push origin main
35	  fi
36	
37	  cd $BASE
38	  rm -rf $CLONE_DIR
39	done

1	import { BUILTIN_REGISTRIES, FALLBACK_STYLE } from "@/src/registry/constants"
2	import { configSchema } from "@/src/schema"
3	import { Config, createConfig, DeepPartial } from "@/src/utils/get-config"
4	import deepmerge from "deepmerge"
5	
6	function resolveStyleFromConfig(config: DeepPartial<Config>) {
7	  if (!config.style) {
8	    return FALLBACK_STYLE
9	  }
10	
11	  // Check if we should use new-york-v4 for Tailwind v4.
12	  // We assume that if tailwind.config is empty, we're using Tailwind v4.
13	  if (config.style === "new-york" && config.tailwind?.config === "") {
14	    return FALLBACK_STYLE
15	  }
16	
17	  return config.style
18	}
19	
20	export function configWithDefaults(config?: DeepPartial<Config>) {
21	  const baseConfig = createConfig({
22	    style: FALLBACK_STYLE,
23	    registries: BUILTIN_REGISTRIES,
24	  })
25	
26	  if (!config) {
27	    return baseConfig
28	  }
29	
30	  return configSchema.parse(
31	    deepmerge(baseConfig, {
32	      ...config,
33	      style: resolveStyleFromConfig(config),
34	      registries: { ...BUILTIN_REGISTRIES, ...config.registries },
35	    })
36	  )
37	}

Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary document. ## Summary Based on my exploration of the shadcn/ui component library codebase, here's a complete understanding of how demo/example files are structured: ### 1. **Example Files Location** - **Primary location:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/` - Contains 235 example files as of this snapshot - Each example is a single `.tsx` file ### 2. **Example File Format & Structure** Example files follow a consistent pattern with these characteristics: **accordion-demo.tsx:** ```typescript import { Accordion, AccordionContent, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion" export default function AccordionDemo() { return ( {/* JSX content */} ) } ``` **tabs-demo.tsx:** ```typescript import { AppWindowIcon, CodeIcon } from "lucide-react" import { Button } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/button" import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardFooter, CardHeader, CardTitle, } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/card" import { Input } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/input" import { Label } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/label" import { Tabs, TabsContent, TabsList, TabsTrigger, } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/tabs" export default function TabsDemo() { return (
{/* JSX content */}
) } ``` ### 3. **Naming Conventions** - **Main demo:** `{component-name}-demo.tsx` (e.g., `accordion-demo.tsx`, `tabs-demo.tsx`) - **Variant examples:** `{component-name}-{variant}.tsx` (e.g., `badge-destructive.tsx`, `badge-outline.tsx`, `button-default.tsx`, `button-ghost.tsx`) - **Multi-component examples:** `{component-name}-{use-case}.tsx` (e.g., `button-group-demo.tsx`, `button-group-dropdown.tsx`, `breadcrumb-dropdown.tsx`) ### 4. **Import Patterns** Examples import from: - **UI components:** `@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/{component}` (primary) - **Icons:** `lucide-react` (for icons) - **React features:** `"use client"` when using client-side features like state (`React.useState`) Import structure is clean, sorted, and only includes what's needed. ### 5. **Default Exports** All example files **export a default function** with a PascalCase name derived from the filename: - `accordion-demo.tsx` → `export default function AccordionDemo()` - `tabs-demo.tsx` → `export default function TabsDemo()` - `button-default.tsx` → `export default function ButtonDefault()` - `button-group-demo.tsx` → `export default function ButtonGroupDemo()` ### 6. **Registry Entries** **Location:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` **Format for UI component (tabs):** ```typescript { name: "tabs", type: "registry:ui", dependencies: ["radix-ui"], files: [ { path: "ui/tabs.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], } ``` **Format for example entry (tabs-demo):** ```typescript { name: "tabs-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["tabs"], files: [ { path: "examples/tabs-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], } ``` **Key differences:** - UI components: `type: "registry:ui"`, `dependencies: [npm packages]`, `files[].type: "registry:ui"` - Examples: `type: "registry:example"`, `registryDependencies: [component names]`, `files[].type: "registry:example"` ### 7. **UI Component Structure** UI components are located at `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/` and follow patterns like: **accordion.tsx:** - Uses `"use client"` directive - Wraps Radix UI primitives (e.g., `Accordion as AccordionPrimitive from "radix-ui"`) - Exports named functions for each sub-component - Uses utility functions like `cn()` from `@/lib/utils` - Applies Tailwind className styling **tabs.tsx:** - Similar pattern with CVA (class-variance-authority) for variants - Exports multiple components: `Tabs`, `TabsList`, `TabsTrigger`, `TabsContent`, `tabsListVariants` ### 8. **Documentation Files** **Location:** `/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/` **Format (accordion.mdx):** ```yaml --- title: Accordion description: A vertically stacked set of interactive headings... base: base component: true links: doc: https://base-ui.com/react/components/accordion api: https://base-ui.com/react/components/accordion#api-reference --- ``` Uses custom MDX components like ``, ``, `` to reference examples and components. **Documentation organization:** - `/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/meta.json` - Lists all documented components - Each component has its own `.mdx` file ### 9. **Registry Build & Generation** **Build command:** `pnpm registry:build` - Runs: `pnpm --filter=shadcn build && bun run ./scripts/build-registry.mts` - Located in `/apps/v4/package.json` - The registry script (`build-registry.mts`) processes all registry entries and generates the index **Scripts directory:** `/apps/v4/scripts/` - `build-registry.mts` - Generates registry index - `build-icons.ts` - Builds icon assets - Other utility scripts **Main registry aggregation file:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/registry.ts` - Imports from: `ui._registry`, `blocks._registry`, `charts._registry`, `examples._registry`, `hooks._registry`, `internal._registry`, `lib._registry` - Combines all registries into a single exported `registry` object - Uses Zod schema validation ### 10. **Stepper/Wizard Conflict Check** Search results for existing stepper/wizard components: **NONE FOUND** - No existing `stepper.tsx` or `wizard.tsx` in `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/` - No existing examples with these names - No conflicts to avoid ### 11. **Configuration Files** **components.json** at `/apps/v4/components.json`: ```json { "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json", "style": "new-york", "rsc": true, "tsx": true, "tailwind": { "config": "", "css": "app/globals.css", "baseColor": "neutral", "cssVariables": true, "prefix": "" }, "aliases": { "components": "@/components", "utils": "@/lib/utils", "ui": "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui", "lib": "@/lib", "hooks": "@/hooks" }, "iconLibrary": "lucide" } ``` --- **Key Takeaways for Implementation:** 1. Create `.tsx` file in `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/` 2. Export a default PascalCase function component 3. Import UI components from `@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/` 4. Add entry to `examples/_registry.ts` with type `"registry:example"` and `registryDependencies` 5. If UI component needed, add to `ui/_registry.ts` with type `"registry:ui"` 6. Add documentation `.mdx` file in `/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/` 7. Update `/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/meta.json` if adding new component docs 8. Run `pnpm registry:build` to generate registry

No existing `stepper.tsx` or `wizard.tsx` in `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/` - No existing examples with these names - No conflicts to avoid ### 11. **Configuration Files** **components.json** at `/apps/v4/components.json`: ```json { "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json", "style": "new-york", "rsc": true, "tsx": true, "tailwind": { "config": "", "css": "app/globals.css", "baseColor": "neutral", "cssVariables": true, "prefix": "" }, "aliases": { "components": "@/components", "utils": "@/lib/utils", "ui": "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui", "lib": "@/lib", "hooks": "@/hooks" }, "iconLibrary": "lucide" } ``` --- **Key Takeaways for Implementation:** 1. Create `.tsx` file in `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/` 2. Export a default PascalCase function component 3. Import UI components from `@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/` 4. Add entry to `examples/_registry.ts` with type `"registry:example"` and `registryDependencies` 5. If UI component needed, add to `ui/_registry.ts` with type `"registry:ui"` 6. Add documentation `.mdx` file in `/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/` 7. Update `/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/meta.json` if adding new component docs 8. Run `pnpm registry:build` to generate registry

Registry System Architecture The registry system in `packages/shadcn/src/registry/` is a sophisticated multi-source component resolution system: #### Core Files: - **`schema.ts`** - Zod schemas defining the entire registry item structure - **`api.ts`** - Public API functions for fetching and resolving registry items - **`resolver.ts`** - Dependency resolution and topological sorting - **`builder.ts`** - URL and header construction for registry items - **`fetcher.ts`** - HTTP fetching with caching - **`parser.ts`** - Parses namespaced item strings (e.g., "@registry/item") - **`config.ts`** - Configuration defaults and merging - **`constants.ts`** - Built-in registries and module lists - **`errors.ts`** - Custom error classes #### Registry Item Types: The system supports multiple registry item types: ``` - registry:base # Base styles/configuration - registry:style # Style definitions - registry:ui # UI components - registry:block # Larger composed blocks - registry:component # Reusable components - registry:hook # Custom React hooks - registry:lib # Utility libraries - registry:page # Full pages - registry:file # Static files - registry:theme # Theme configurations - registry:font # Font definitions - registry:example # Demo examples - registry:internal # Internal use only ``` ### 3. Registry File Structure and Format #### Main Registry Schema (from `schema.ts`): Each registry item has this structure: ```typescript { name: string // Unique identifier type: RegistryItemType // One of the types listed above title?: string // Display title description?: string // Description author?: string // Author info dependencies?: string[] // npm dependencies devDependencies?: string[] // npm dev dependencies registryDependencies?: string[] // Other components it needs files: Array<{ path: string // Source path type: RegistryItemType // File type target?: string // Installation target content?: string // Optional inline content }> tailwind?: { config?: { content?: string[] theme?: Record plugins?: string[] } } cssVars?: { // CSS variable definitions theme?: Record light?: Record dark?: Record } css?: Record // Custom CSS meta?: Record // Arbitrary metadata docs?: string // Documentation markdown categories?: string[] // Component categories extends?: string // Inheritance } ``` #### Registry Format: ```typescript { name: "shadcn/ui" homepage: "https://ui.shadcn.com" items: RegistryItem[] } ``` ### 4. Registry Structure in `apps/v4/registry/` The actual component registry is organized as: ``` apps/v4/registry/ ├── new-york-v4/ # Current style (Tailwind v4) │ ├── ui/ # Base UI components │ │ ├── _registry.ts # UI component definitions │ │ ├── accordion.tsx # Component implementations │ │ ├── button.tsx │ │ ├── card.tsx │ │ ├── sidebar.tsx # Complex compound components │ │ └── ... (59 total UI components) │ │ │ ├── examples/ # Demo/example components │ │ ├── _registry.ts # Example definitions │ │ ├── accordion-demo.tsx │ │ ├── button-default.tsx │ │ └── ... (250+ examples) │ │ │ ├── blocks/ # Complex pre-built blocks │ │ ├── _registry.ts # Block definitions │ │ ├── dashboard-01/ │ │ │ ├── page.tsx # Main block page │ │ │ ├── data.json # Sample data │ │ │ └── components/ # Block sub-components │ │ │ ├── app-sidebar.tsx │ │ │ ├── chart-area-interactive.tsx │ │ │ └── ... (multiple components per block) │ │ ├── sidebar-01/ │ │ └── ... (30+ blocks) │ │ │ ├── charts/ # Chart components │ │ ├── _registry.ts │ │ └── ... (70+ chart examples) │ │ │ ├── hooks/ # Custom hooks │ │ ├── _registry.ts │ │ └── use-mobile.ts │ │ │ ├── lib/ # Utility libraries │ │ ├── _registry.ts │ │ └── utils.ts │ │ │ ├── internal/ # Internal utilities │ │ └── _registry.ts │ │ │ ├── registry.ts # Master registry combining all categories │ ├── themes.ts # Theme definitions │ ├── base-colors.ts # Base color themes │ ├── styles.tsx # Style registry │ └── config.ts # Configuration │ ├── bases/ # Base configurations ├── styles/ # Style definitions ├── icons/ # Icon libraries ├── registry.json # Pre-built JSON registry (output) └── directory.json # Component directory ``` ### 5. Registry Building Process The registry is built from TypeScript files using this pattern: **File: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`** ```typescript import { type Registry } from "shadcn/schema" export const ui: Registry["items"] = [ { name: "button", type: "registry:ui", dependencies: ["radix-ui"], files: [ { path: "ui/button.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], }, // ... more items ] ``` **Master registry: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/registry.ts`** ```typescript export const registry = { name: "shadcn/ui", homepage: "https://ui.shadcn.com", items: z.array(registryItemSchema).parse([ { name: "index", ...NEW_YORK_V4_STYLE }, { name: "style", ...NEW_YORK_V4_STYLE }, ...ui, ...blocks, ...charts, ...lib, ...hooks, ...themes, ...examples, ...internal, ].filter((item) => !DEPRECATED_ITEMS.includes(item.name))) } satisfies Registry ``` ### 6. Compound Component Patterns The library uses multiple patterns for compound components: #### Pattern 1: Sub-components without Context (Simple Compound) **Example: `Card` component** ```typescript // File: card.tsx function Card({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) { return
} function CardHeader({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) { return
} function CardTitle({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) { return
} function CardDescription({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) { return
} function CardContent({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) { return
} function CardFooter({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) { return
} function CardAction({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) { return
} export { Card, CardHeader, CardFooter, CardTitle, CardAction, CardDescription, CardContent, } ``` Exported as separate named exports without a dot notation pattern. #### Pattern 2: Radix UI Primitives with Wrapper Sub-components **Example: `Accordion` component** ```typescript function Accordion({ ...props }: React.ComponentProps) { return } function AccordionItem({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps) { return } function AccordionTrigger({ className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps) { return ( {children} ) } function AccordionContent({ className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps) { return (
{children}
 ) } export { Accordion, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, AccordionContent } ``` #### Pattern 3: React Context-Based Compound Components **Example: `Sidebar` component (most complex)** ```typescript // Create context for state management type SidebarContextProps = { state: "expanded" | "collapsed" open: boolean setOpen: (open: boolean) => void openMobile: boolean setOpenMobile: (open: boolean) => void isMobile: boolean toggleSidebar: () => void } const SidebarContext = React.createContext(null) // Custom hook to access context function useSidebar() { const context = React.useContext(SidebarContext) if (!context) { throw new Error("useSidebar must be used within a SidebarProvider.") } return context } // Provider component function SidebarProvider({ defaultOpen = true, open: openProp, onOpenChange: setOpenProp, className, style, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & { defaultOpen?: boolean open?: boolean onOpenChange?: (open: boolean) => void }) { const isMobile = useIsMobile() const [openMobile, setOpenMobile] = React.useState(false) const [_open, _setOpen] = React.useState(defaultOpen) // ... context setup return (
{children}
) } // Sub-component using context function Sidebar({ side = "left", variant = "sidebar", collapsible = "offcanvas", className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & {...}) { const { isMobile, state, openMobile, setOpenMobile } = useSidebar() // ... implementation } export { SidebarProvider, Sidebar, useSidebar, ... } ``` **Key Pattern Characteristics:** - No dot notation (`Component.SubComponent`) - Use React Context for state sharing - Custom hooks (`useSidebar`) for accessing context - Throws helpful error if used outside provider - Cookie-based state persistence in browser ### 7. Demo/Example File Structure Examples follow a simple naming convention: **File: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts`** ```typescript export const examples: Registry["items"] = [ { name: "accordion-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["accordion"], files: [ { path: "examples/accordion-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], }, // ... more examples ] ``` **Example file: `accordion-demo.tsx`** ```typescript import { Accordion, AccordionContent, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion" export default function AccordionDemo() { return ( Is it accessible? Yes. It adheres to the WAI-ARIA design pattern. {/* ... more items */} ) } ``` ### 8. Configuration Files #### `components.json` (Project Configuration) Located at: `apps/v4/components.json` ```json { "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json", "style": "new-york", "rsc": true, "tsx": true, "tailwind": { "config": "", "css": "app/globals.css", "baseColor": "neutral", "cssVariables": true, "prefix": "" }, "aliases": { "components": "@/components", "utils": "@/lib/utils", "ui": "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui", "lib": "@/lib", "hooks": "@/hooks" }, "iconLibrary": "lucide" } ``` #### Root `package.json` Configuration - Workspaces: `apps/*` and `packages/*` - Package manager: pnpm@9.0.6 - Key scripts: `registry:build`, `registry:capture`, `validate:registries` - Type: ESM modules ### 9. Complex Block Structure Blocks are pre-built, feature-complete compositions: **Example: Dashboard Block** ``` blocks/dashboard-01/ ├── page.tsx # Main page component ├── data.json # Sample data └── components/ ├── app-sidebar.tsx # Sidebar with navigation ├── chart-area-interactive.tsx # Interactive chart ├── data-table.tsx # Data table component ├── nav-documents.tsx ├── nav-main.tsx ├── nav-secondary.tsx ├── nav-user.tsx ├── section-cards.tsx └── site-header.tsx ``` **Block Registry Entry:** ```typescript { name: "dashboard-01", type: "registry:block", description: "A dashboard with sidebar, charts and data table.", dependencies: [ "@dnd-kit/core", "@tabler/icons-react", "@tanstack/react-table", "zod", ], registryDependencies: [ "sidebar", "breadcrumb", "chart", "card", "table", // ... many more ], files: [ { path: "blocks/dashboard-01/page.tsx", type: "registry:page", target: "app/dashboard/page.tsx", // Installation target }, { path: "blocks/dashboard-01/data.json", type: "registry:file", target: "app/dashboard/data.json", }, { path: "blocks/dashboard-01/components/app-sidebar.tsx", type: "registry:component", }, // ... more component files ], categories: ["dashboard"], meta: { iframeHeight: "1000px", }, } ``` ### 10. Key Registry System Features #### Dependency Resolution The `resolver.ts` implements: - **Recursive dependency resolution** - Automatically pulls in required dependencies - **Topological sorting** - Ensures components are installed in correct order using Kahn's algorithm - **Circular dependency detection** - Handles circular dependencies gracefully - **Source tracking** - Tracks which registry each item comes from - **Deduplication** - Prevents duplicate installations #### Multiple Registry Support - **Built-in registries** - `@shadcn` pointing to UI registry - **Custom registries** - Configured via `components.json` - **Namespaced items** - `@registry-name/component-name` syntax - **URL-based items** - Direct HTTP URLs or local file paths - **Authentication** - Supports headers and query params for authenticated registries #### Theme Management - **Base colors** - neutral, stone, zinc, mauve, olive, mist, taupe - **CSS variables** - Theme, light, and dark variants - **Tailwind integration** - Config merging and theme token generation - **v3/v4 support** - Detects Tailwind version and applies appropriate styles ### 11. Entry Points (package.json exports) The `shadcn` package exports: - `.` - Main CLI and functions - `./registry` - Registry API and utilities - `./schema` - Zod schemas and types - `./mcp` - Model Context Protocol integration - `./utils` - Utility functions - `./icons` - Icon sets - `./preset` - Component presets - `./tailwind.css` - Base CSS --- **Summary:** The shadcn/ui architecture is a sophisticated, scalable system that separates component definitions (registry) from implementations, supports multiple component types and styles, uses context for complex compound components, and implements advanced dependency resolution with topological sorting. Components are registered as JSON-serializable objects with file paths and metadata, allowing automated installation and configuration management.

Complex Block Structure Blocks are pre-built, feature-complete compositions: **Example: Dashboard Block** ``` blocks/dashboard-01/ ├── page.tsx # Main page component ├── data.json # Sample data └── components/ ├── app-sidebar.tsx # Sidebar with navigation ├── chart-area-interactive.tsx # Interactive chart ├── data-table.tsx # Data table component ├── nav-documents.tsx ├── nav-main.tsx ├── nav-secondary.tsx ├── nav-user.tsx ├── section-cards.tsx └── site-header.tsx ``` **Block Registry Entry:** ```typescript { name: "dashboard-01", type: "registry:block", description: "A dashboard with sidebar, charts and data table.", dependencies: [ "@dnd-kit/core", "@tabler/icons-react", "@tanstack/react-table", "zod", ], registryDependencies: [ "sidebar", "breadcrumb", "chart", "card", "table", // ... many more ], files: [ { path: "blocks/dashboard-01/page.tsx", type: "registry:page", target: "app/dashboard/page.tsx", // Installation target }, { path: "blocks/dashboard-01/data.json", type: "registry:file", target: "app/dashboard/data.json", }, { path: "blocks/dashboard-01/components/app-sidebar.tsx", type: "registry:component", }, // ... more component files ], categories: ["dashboard"], meta: { iframeHeight: "1000px", }, } ``` ### 10. Key Registry System Features #### Dependency Resolution The `resolver.ts` implements: - **Recursive dependency resolution** - Automatically pulls in required dependencies - **Topological sorting** - Ensures components are installed in correct order using Kahn's algorithm - **Circular dependency detection** - Handles circular dependencies gracefully - **Source tracking** - Tracks which registry each item comes from - **Deduplication** - Prevents duplicate installations #### Multiple Registry Support - **Built-in registries** - `@shadcn` pointing to UI registry - **Custom registries** - Configured via `components.json` - **Namespaced items** - `@registry-name/component-name` syntax - **URL-based items** - Direct HTTP URLs or local file paths - **Authentication** - Supports headers and query params for authenticated registries #### Theme Management - **Base colors** - neutral, stone, zinc, mauve, olive, mist, taupe - **CSS variables** - Theme, light, and dark variants - **Tailwind integration** - Config merging and theme token generation - **v3/v4 support** - Detects Tailwind version and applies appropriate styles ### 11. Entry Points (package.json exports) The `shadcn` package exports: - `.` - Main CLI and functions - `./registry` - Registry API and utilities - `./schema` - Zod schemas and types - `./mcp` - Model Context Protocol integration - `./utils` - Utility functions - `./icons` - Icon sets - `./preset` - Component presets - `./tailwind.css` - Base CSS --- **Summary:** The shadcn/ui architecture is a sophisticated, scalable system that separates component definitions (registry) from implementations, supports multiple component types and styles, uses context for complex compound components, and implements advanced dependency resolution with topological sorting. Components are registered as JSON-serializable objects with file paths and metadata, allowing automated installation and configuration management.

Let me now think about the plan for the Stepper component: ## Plan ### Files to create: 1. `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` - The main Stepper component 2. `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx` - The demo example ### Files to modify: 1. `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` - Add stepper entry 2. `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` - Add stepper-demo entry ### Component Design I need to build a Stepper component with a root container that manages context and orientation, individual step items that track their number and status, clickable triggers, titles, descriptions, and separators between steps. ### Context Design The root Stepper context should hold the active step, change callback, and orientation setting. Each StepperItem will have its own context layer that derives the step status by comparing its step number against the active step. This two-level context approach keeps concerns separated cleanly. ### API Design Building the component API with a controlled value pattern... Reconsidering the separator placement — it shouldn't live inside StepperItem since it connects items rather than belonging to one. Let me think through the structural options... I'm leaning toward a structure where the stepper list wraps the step indicators with their triggers and separators, while the content sections live separately below—this keeps the layout flexible and makes it easier to manage state across a wizard flow. Building the component structure... I'm thinking through the context design now. The root context needs to track the current step value and a callback for when it changes, plus the orientation. For each item, I need to determine its status—whether it's active, completed, or pending—which requires knowing not just its own value but also where it sits relative to all other steps in the sequence. The tricky part is figuring out step order. I could use numeric step props, track registration order as items mount, or have the user explicitly pass completion state. The cleanest approach that matches the existing patterns would be to have items register themselves with an ordered list of values, or use the context to compare step positions—but I need to figure out how to maintain that order information without making it too complex. I think numeric steps is the way to go—it's straightforward and works well for wizard-like flows. The `Stepper` component holds the active step number, each `StepperItem` compares its step prop to determine its status, and `StepperContent` simply shows or hides based on whether its step matches the current one. Building out the full component API... I'm working through the visual design for the stepper component now. For a horizontal layout, the steps sit in a row with circle indicators connected by separator lines, and the content displays below. In vertical mode, the steps stack with a vertical line connecting them, and content can appear alongside or beneath each step. I need to handle different indicator states—active steps show a filled circle with the number, completed ones display a checkmark, and pending steps show an empty circle. Building the DOM structure... I'm realizing the separator should live inside each `StepperItem` at the end, so users can control which steps display them—typically all except the last one. The `StepperSeparator` component will adapt its orientation visually based on context, rendering as a horizontal line for horizontal steppers and a vertical line for vertical ones. Now I'm thinking through the Tailwind classes needed for the main `Stepper` container and how to use data attributes to handle the orientation switching. Defining the TypeScript interfaces... Writing component styles... I'm working through the layout structure for both orientations—horizontally, each item sits in a row with its trigger and separator taking up space, while vertically, the trigger and separator stack within each item, with the indicator and text positioned side by side. Writing component markup... Writing the indicator styles... I'm realizing the `StepperTrigger` should pull the step number and status from context rather than relying on props, which keeps the component clean and self-contained. The trigger will handle the click handler to update the active step, and it'll render both the indicator circle and any children passed to it. But I'm hitting a design question—if the trigger auto-renders the indicator and wraps children in a specific layout, that limits customization. Maybe the cleaner approach is to let the trigger just style the button itself and let users control the layout of their content. Though the indicator still needs to automatically show the right state—a number or checkmark depending on progress. Looking at how other design systems handle steppers, they typically auto-render the indicator inside the trigger as the first child, which gives users a sensible default while keeping the API simple. That feels like the right balance here. So I'm settling on this: `StepperTrigger` automatically renders the step indicator, and users can optionally add `StepperTitle` and `StepperDescription` as children. The layout then adapts based on orientation—horizontal puts the indicator on top with text below, while vertical stacks the indicator on the left with text flowing to the right. Building the trigger component with orientation-aware styling... Writing component styles... Actually, I'm reconsidering the children wrapper—in vertical mode, I should just pass children directly without the extra div so users have full control over their layout instead of forcing a flex column structure. For horizontal mode, the children stack naturally since `StepperTitle` and `StepperDescription` are block-level elements. The real difference is positioning: vertical needs the indicator and text side-by-side with `flex-row`, while horizontal stacks everything vertically with `flex-col`. So the trigger itself should handle the layout direction, and the title plus description will arrange themselves accordingly without needing a wrapper. and description need to be in a sub-column in vertical mode... they can't just be flex children of the trigger in flex-row mode and look right. OK I think I need to either: 1. Wrap title+description in a div inside the trigger automatically 2. Or use CSS grid Actually, for vertical mode the title and description are just text that stacks naturally since they're `display: block` elements. In a `flex-row` trigger, they'd each be their own column which is wrong. I think the cleanest solution here is to make `StepperTitle` and `StepperDescription` use `display: block` (which is default for `

`), and when the trigger is `flex-col`, they stack. When the trigger is `flex-row`, we need them grouped together. One approach: in the trigger, render the indicator and then a `

` wrapper for the rest. But then the user needs to put their title and description inside a sub-component that handles this grouping. This is getting complex. Let me simplify by having `StepperTrigger` render the indicator circle, then wrap the children in a container that keeps the title and description grouped together regardless of the flex direction.
